body_txt: Seals have long enjoyed a reputation for cuteness , playfulness and for just being all around quirky and lovable sea critters, essentially the furrier, semi-aquatic version of dolphins . Until you realize that they have a mouthful of sharp fangs and are actually formidable predators (again, not unlike dolphins ), and predators aren't exactly the most beloved animals . Pinnipeds are, after all, literally the bears and wolves of the sea, since they are fellow caniform carnivorans. Many species also reach rather intimidating sizes, with even smaller species weighing more than a grown man. So when they're not Ridiculously Cute Critters , fictional seals are often portrayed as either mindless brutes or malevolent monsters. Due to being the chief natural predator of penguins , the leopard seal has been a popular pick as the antagonist in penguin-centric stories for the last few decades, showing the small cuddly bird being pursued by a 12-foot ravenous killer ( with some portrayals exaggerating its size to be on par with a great white or even an orca ). Besides leopard seals, other pinnipeds like walruses, sea lions, and especially elephant seals are renowned for their very intimidating size, as they are the largest known carnivorans, outsizing even the biggest bears ( to the point that polar bears struggle to kill a walrus and have to look out for its tusks ). A bull southern elephant seal might tip the scales at 4 tons! It doesn't help that many giant pinnipeds are also capable of uttering very deep, guttural roars every bit as scary as a lion or bear's. This trope mostly applies to large, stereotypically aggressive seal species such as leopard seals and elephant seals. Small ones, such as the harbor seal and the harp seal, are much more likely to be portrayed as Sweet Seals . If a small seal does act evil or aggressive in fiction, it's a case of Killer Rabbit . There's some aspects of What Measure Is a Non-Cute? involved here too; generally, the larger seals that prey on penguins and other seals are portrayed more negatively in fiction than the smaller ones that mostly eat fish, and fish aren't usually seen as cute or appealing enough to feel sorry for . This trope also includes sea lions. They're different from seals — for instance, they have ear flaps, and flippers that allow them to "walk" on land — but are also included due to being highly similar to them in most if not all other respects; in fact, the entirety of the immediate clade that includes them both consists of marine mammals of the same general body plan and carnivorous behavior, including walruses and fur seals (the latter being the sea lions' closest relatives). And their closest relatives? Bears . Compare Wily Walrus (since walruses are related to seals), Threatening Shark , Cats Are Mean and Devious Dolphins . Contrast Sweet Seal . Sub-Trope of Sea Monster . Not to Be Confused with Sealed Evil in a Can or seals on letters. Also not to be confused with Navy SEALs , although their targets may very well be scared. Has nothing to do with the singer Seal .
